meeting_type: 10.3.14 Confidential litigation update - October 1 to December 31, 2020 (L-06-21)
tldr: Burlington's Corporate Services committee met for over 12 hours to discuss the 2021 operating budget, with two public delegations speaking on budget matters. The main budget item was deferred to a special meeting on February 25, while the committee also approved a COVID enforcement funding agreement with regional partners.

items.title: Memorandum of Understanding for COVID enforcement funding
items.notes: Committee authorized the Mayor and City Clerk to negotiate and execute an agreement with regional municipalities for COVID-19 enforcement framework and one-time funding.

items.title: 2021 budget multi-year simulation
items.notes: Finance department report on multi-year budget projections was received and filed, with item scheduled for special council meeting on March 3.

items.title: 2021 operating budget review and approval
items.notes: The main 2021 operating budget item was referred to a special committee budget meeting on February 25 after hearing from two public delegations.

items.title: Motion to proceed into closed session - budget compensation matters
items.notes: Committee moved into closed session to discuss budget reductions related to compensation under labour relations provisions.

items.title: Motion to proceed into closed session - personnel and staffing matters
items.notes: Second closed session motion to discuss personal matters about employees and staffing requests related to budget items.

items.title: Information items - correspondence and delegation notes
items.notes: Committee received and filed correspondence about City View Park and delegation notes from Lawson Hunter regarding the operating budget.
